name: svgpng
summary: Encode an RGBA image (rasterized from an SVG by the canvas) into compressed PNG bytes.
note: SVG->pixels rasterization is done by the native browser canvas (not WASM). This kernel does the compute-bound encode: PNG 'Up' filter + Adler-32 + DEFLATE (fixed Huffman + greedy LZ77). Output is a zlib stream = the PNG IDAT payload; the engine wraps it in PNG chunks (signature/IHDR/IDAT/IEND + CRC-32) in JS.

memory.note: linear memory, exported for host marshaling

reset.desc: Rewind the bump allocator before laying out buffers for a new image.

alloc.desc: Reserve byteLen bytes (8-byte aligned), growing memory as needed.

params.name: pixelsPtr
params.desc: byte ptr to u8 RGBA pixels[height*width*4]

params.name: filteredPtr
params.desc: scratch u8[height*(1+width*4)] for the filtered stream

params.name: headPtr
params.desc: scratch i32[32768] hash heads (cleared by the kernel)

params.name: outPtr
params.desc: byte ptr for the zlib stream (OUTPUT); size >= filteredLen*2 + 1024

encode.desc: Filter + Adler-32 + DEFLATE the image in one pass; writes a zlib stream (2-byte header + DEFLATE + 4-byte Adler-32) to outPtr and returns its length.

memoryLayout.name: filtered
memoryLayout.desc: PNG-filtered scanlines (filter byte + Up-filtered row), also the DEFLATE input.

memoryLayout.name: head/prev
memoryLayout.desc: LZ77 hash-chain tables. head is cleared each call; prev is written before it is read.

memoryLayout.name: out
memoryLayout.desc: zlib stream (the PNG IDAT payload). The host reads outLen bytes and wraps them in PNG chunks.

marshaling.rule: Call reset(), then alloc() every buffer up front (alloc may grow memory and detach views), then snapshot the Uint8Array view over memory.buffer, then write pixels. After init no re-grow happens, so repeated encode() calls reuse the same views.
marshaling.boundaryCrossings: 1 wasm call per image; payload is the whole image (chunky, not chatty). Only outLen bytes come back.
